articles.title: CANADA'S CRYPTO TAX CRACKDOWN
articles.summary: Canada's tax agency (CRA) has pulled in over $100M CAD (~$72M) from crypto audits in the last three years, but hasn't brought a criminal case to court since 2020. Although it estimates ~40% of users on platforms that it sees are non-compliant or high-risk, the CRA admits it still can't reliably identify most crypto users. As part of only its second-ever crypto data dragnet, the CRA forced Dapper Labs to hand over info on 2,500 users, following an earlier order against Coinsquare. The gap between money collected and cases prosecuted stems from cross-border complexity, scarce evidence, and slow international cooperation, even as Canada gears up a new financial-crimes agency and continues big AML fines via FINTRAC.

articles.title: POLAND LEFT AS MICA OUTLIER IN THE EU
articles.summary: Poland's parliament failed to override the president's veto of its MiCA-alignment bill, leaving the country as the only EU member without a domestic MiCA framework. Germany, Malta, the Netherlands, and Lithuania are already issuing MiCA licenses. However, Poland's crypto market is booming anyway. It ranks 8th in Europe by on-chain value, with >50% YoY volume growth and ~7.9M people using crypto. Meanwhile, Italy is warning firms they must be fully MiCA-compliant or shut down by December 30, and Brussels is exploring a future model where a single EU-level supervisor regulates exchanges across the bloc, which could eventually reduce the impact of national holdouts like Poland.

articles.title: METAMASK INTEGRATES POLYMARKET
articles.summary: MetaMask has integrated Polymarket into its mobile app, enabling one-tap funding with any token on any EVM chain and MetaMask Rewards points toward a potential MASK airdrop as Consensys reportedly plans an IPO. The integration provides a new access point for Polymarket, which recently secured CFTC approval to re-enter the US and is courting a $15 billion valuation after receiving $2 billion in strategic funding from NYSE parent ICE at a $9 billion valuation.

articles.title: KRAKEN LAUNCHES HIGH-TOUCH VIP PROGRAM
articles.summary: Kraken launched an invitation-only VIP program that requires $10 million average balances or $80 million annual trading volume, pairing ultra-high-net-worth clients with dedicated relationship managers that provide 24/7 support, subject-matter expert access, and exclusive Formula 1, football, and cultural experiences ahead of its early 2026 US IPO. The high-touch service competes with Binance's recent "prestige" offering for clients up to $10 million net worth as exchanges fill the wealth management gap for family offices and sophisticated traders.

articles.title: BITCOIN FACES JAPAN RATE HIKE: DEBUNKING THE YEN CARRY TRADE
articles.summary: Fears of a yen carry trade unwind from the Bank of Japan's expected 0.75% rate hike are overblown as the move is already priced into Japanese government bond yields near multi-decade highs, and speculators hold net long yen positions, limiting panic buying potential, contrasting with August 2024's bearish positioning that triggered market chaos. The yield differential between Japan's 0.75% and the US' 3.75% remains wide enough to favor American assets and discourage mass carry trade unwinding, with BOJ staying the most dovish major central bank. The real risk lies in Japanese tightening sustaining elevated US Treasury yields that counter Fed rate cuts, dampening global risk appetite by raising borrowing costs, rather than a sudden yen surge, while Trump's fiscal expansion push could stoke debt fears, lifting bond yields further.

articles.title: MARKET ECHOES EARLY 2022 AS ONCHAIN STRESS MOUNTS
NYT'S “BANK DEPOSIT VS STABLECOIN WALLET” GRAPHIC BACKLASH
articles.summary: A New York Times infographic contrasts bank deposits (insured, interest-bearing, with legal rights) against stablecoin balances (uninsured, issuer keeps yield, weaker legal claims). However, this is a stacked comparison as banks historically share very little of the yield they earn, many countries have weak or no deposit insurance, and large depositors are just unsecured creditors of leveraged banks. In crypto, users can move stablecoins into DeFi or tokenized T-bill products to capture much higher yields directly, and self-custody avoids many of the freezes and capital controls that can hit bank accounts.

articles.title: CRYPTO IS GOING MAINSTREAM
articles.summary: Crypto is quietly integrating as a secure communication layer between applications rather than through dominant L1s like Ethereum or Solana. Stablecoins already process $9 trillion adjusted annual volume, rivaling Visa and PayPal by representing fiat balances on interoperable blockchain transport. Apps like PayPal and LINE Pay are operating as independent L2s posting ordered transactions to L1s using erasure coding, where each validator handles constant data loads regardless of scale. Monad excitement reflects outdated mental models, as fast finality became table stakes and developer lock-in to monolithic VMs contradicts a decade of experience showing easy EVM portability, while unified liquidity emerges from universal base layers enabling secure communication across chains rather than single blockchains attempting everything.

articles.title: ETHEREUM ‘KILLER' VS ETHEREUM L2 REALITY
articles.summary: From 2019 to 2024, Solana marketed itself as the scalable, monolithic alternative to “band-aid” L2s, but by 2024 to 2025, Base was leading L2s in TVL growth, daily active users, and meme/creator activity, even overtaking Solana in new token launches and associated DEX volume in August.

articles.title: ETHEREUM FEES & STABLECOIN LIQUIDITY
articles.summary: Median transaction fees on Ethereum are at all-time lows, even as dollar stablecoin supply on Ethereum hits all-time highs.

articles.title: ZKSYNC LITE DEPRECATION IN 2026
articles.summary: ZKsync is planning to shut down ZKsync Lite (its original 2020-era ZK rollup) sometime in 2026, calling it a completed “proof-of-concept” that paved the way for newer products like ZKsync Era, ZK Stack chains, and Prividiums.

articles.title: RAINBOW'S UNIQUE TOKEN MODEL
articles.summary: Rainbow is giving 20% of Rainbow Company's equity to the Rainbow Foundation so that RNBW token holders and employees can be better aligned.
